message:
test(conformance_mcp_manifest): add resource-template manifest conformance suite + beads

## New conformance surface: MCP resource templates

The existing conformance_mcp_manifest test suite covered tools,
resources, and prompts via ManifestListResponse, but did not cover
resource templates — the MCP surface that advertises parameterized
resource URIs like `ft://events/{limit}` or `ft://rules/{agent_type}`.
Any change to the server's resource_templates() implementation (new
templates, renamed URI patterns, changed mimeType) would slip past
the existing suite undetected.

Add a parallel set of types, parsers, and tests specifically for
resource templates.

### New types (#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] throughout)

  - ResourceTemplateManifestResponse { resourceTemplates: Vec<_> }
  - ResourceTemplateListEntry { uriTemplate, name, description,
                                 mimeType, version, tags }

deny_unknown_fields is the critical ingredient — it ensures the test
suite catches any extra field the server accidentally starts emitting
(the "extra_field" negative case would fail without it).

### New helpers (`fn`s)

  - capture_resource_template_lists(db_path) — hits the live server via
    build_server_with_db, collects resource_templates(), sorts by
    uriTemplate, and returns the canonical JSON value.
  - parse_resource_template_manifest(value) — deserializes + validates.
  - validate_resource_template_manifest(manifest) — uniqueness check
    on template URIs via BTreeSet.

### New tests (4)

  - live_server_resource_templates_conform_with_and_without_db:
    end-to-end, asserts both the ephemeral (no-db) and db-backed
    surfaces parse cleanly and that the db-backed surface is at
    least as large as the ephemeral one.

  - conformance_resource_template_valid_cases_cover_minimal_and_
    optional_fields: 3 valid fixtures — empty list, fully-populated
    template with all optional fields, and explicit-null optional
    fields. Each parses without error.

  - conformance_resource_template_invalid_cases_reject_shape_and_
    uniqueness_breaks: 6 negative fixtures pinning missing field,
    wrong type for resourceTemplates, duplicate URIs, missing
    uriTemplate on a specific entry, wrong mimeType type, and
    deny-unknown-fields rejection of an "oops" field. Each asserts
    the error message contains a specific substring for grep-ability.

## Beads

- File ft-3j0s0 (open, P2 feature): "tmux_cc parser chunk-boundary
  regression coverage". Placeholder for a follow-up track that adds
  similar conformance coverage for the tmux_cc wire-format parser.

- Close ft-8uj10: "Already landed in 765743d5 (fix(events): sort
  extracted object keys before event identity hash + ft-gc4hz
  circuit-breaker test)." Bead was filed in the same session as its
  fix but the closure slipped; this commit catches it up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-3khc2","title":"[mock] tui/ftui_stub.rs:1578 render_view_placeholder is dead code","description":"tui/ftui_stub.rs:1578 fn render_view_placeholder() renders "[FTUI migration in progress — {view} view not yet ported]" to users. It is marked #[allow(dead_code)] because all views have been migrated (line 13 of the file says "no placeholders remain"). This function has zero callers — it is leftover migration scaffolding. Production behavior should be: delete the dead render_view_placeholder function (lines 1574-1608) since all FTUI views are now implemented and the function is unreachable.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:46.632782Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.111075Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.110745Z","close_reason":"done — removed dead render_view_placeholder (0 callers, all views migrated) (d83d30d4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-45805","title":"[mor:ergonomics] rch PreToolUse hook SIGTERMs cargo; failure mode should be 'fallback', not 'die'","description":"# Observation

From working memory + user experience across recent sessions:

- rch PreToolUse hook (\`force_remote=true\` in
  ~/.config/rch/config.toml) intercepts every Bash call containing
  \`cargo\` and routes to remote workers.
- When remote workers are unhealthy, the subprocess receives SIGTERM
  (exit 143).
- Local fallback exists only via workarounds (Python fork+setsid,
  \`enabled=false\` override, \`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<name>\`
  bypass).

At 3am: operator needs to reproduce a test failure. Runs
\`cargo test foo\`. Shell returns exit 143 with no diagnostic.
Operator has to know the workaround exists. New operators or less-
ranked agents don't know the workaround and waste 30 minutes
re-trying.

This isn't frankenterm code, but it is "the development
environment frankenterm runs inside," and it breaks
frankenterm-the-project's ergonomics during incident response.

# Question

Two levers worth exploring:

1. **Fallback-on-worker-failure in rch itself.** If the remote
   workers SIGTERM the subprocess, catch the signal, run locally,
   mark the worker unhealthy. Exit 143 becomes a last resort,
   not the default failure mode.

2. **Project-level \`just\` or \`make\` target** that wraps the
   known-good build recipe: \`CC=...clang CXX=...clang++
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-local-target cargo <args>\`. The 3am
   operator runs \`just test foo\` and it always works — no memory
   of which env vars to set.

Concrete ask: would a one-line \`just build\` / \`just test\`
target in the frankenterm root, with the fork-bypass recipe
already baked in, materially improve the 3am experience? It's
~15 min of work and saves 30 min per incident per operator.

# 3am principle

Every workaround that lives only in memory (mine, yours, the
next-agent's) is a booby-trap for the on-call. Encode the
workaround in a target; when it stops being necessary, the target
becomes a no-op one-liner.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:41.686932Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.612291Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.611695Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","mor","rch","tooling"]}
@@ -32,7 +33,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-7axpo","title":"protocol_recovery deterministic jitter defeats thundering-herd protection","description":"# [HIGH] protocol_recovery: fake jitter

**Subsystem:** protocol_recovery / retry
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/protocol_recovery.rs (`RecoveryConfig::delay_for_attempt`)

## The hole

\`\`\`rust
let jitter_seed = ((attempt as f64 * 7.13).sin().abs()).mul_add(2.0, -1.0);
let jittered_ms = jitter_range.mul_add(jitter_seed, capped_ms).max(1.0);
\`\`\`

`sin(attempt * 7.13)` is fully deterministic. Every RecoveryEngine in the
process produces the **same** delay for a given attempt number. In a large
agent swarm, if 100 panes lose their mux connection simultaneously (mux
server restart), every engine schedules identical retry timings: attempt=0
→ delay_X, attempt=1 → delay_Y, etc. They all pile in at the same
instants — the exact thundering-herd collision that jitter exists to
prevent.

Pre-fix: `jitter_fraction: 0.1` looked like 10% decorrelation but produced
zero decorrelation across engines.

## Fix

Replaced the deterministic `sin()`-derived seed with
`rand::rng().random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range)` — same approach
already used in `retry.rs::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt`. Consistent with
how the other module in the same crate spells jitter.

Kept the `jitter_range > 0.0` fast-path so `jitter_fraction == 0.0` is
still bit-exact deterministic (tests rely on this).

## Caller impact

`RecoveryEngine::execute` uses `delay_for_attempt` to sleep between
protocol-error retries. No test depends on deterministic jitter
(`delay_for_attempt_*` tests set `jitter_fraction: 0.0`), so the fix is
drop-in.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:26.938808Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102198Z","close_reason":"Fix already shipped in fdd1e817; regression tests added in bf4a63b9 (delay_for_attempt_jitter_is_randomized_across_calls + delay_for_attempt_zero_jitter_is_deterministic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["protocol-recovery","reliability","retry"]}
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8jbpj","title":"wezterm.rs pre-existing compile breakage blocks ft-e34d9.10.5.2","description":"While validating ft-e34d9.10.5.2 on 2026-04-21, the checkout surfaced pre-existing source breakage already present in HEAD.

Primary blocker:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:1963-1968 contains a malformed pool_result branch. After the explicit-Cx else arm calls pool.send_paste_with_cx(&cx, pane_id, data).await, HEAD still has a stray pool.send_paste(pane_id, data).await line before the branch closes. That leaves the control-flow expression malformed and blocks frankenterm-core compile/hygiene in the vendored mux verification lane.

Related hygiene blocker surfaced in the same lane:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1891 contains a stray ); inside PaneOutputSubscription, so rustfmt cannot parse the file while formatting touched vendored mux changes.

Evidence:
- dirty diff against HEAD shows removing the stray send_paste line in wezterm.rs and the stray ); in mux_client.rs restores parsable source shape locally.
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p7-orch-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cancellation_during_ --features asupersync-runtime,vendored -- --nocapture surfaced pre-existing frankenterm-core compile failures during the same validation lane.

This bug is blocking clean verification progress on ft-e34d9.10.5.2 support work and should be fixed before further vendored mux migration validation claims are made.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:15.535866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556225Z","close_reason":"Fixed by 9e1bd63b (ft-xbnl0.2.5 bulk cfg strip) — stray pool.send_paste and mux_client.rs stray ); both removed with the non-cx fallback branches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754838Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.501104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.500840Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:
- machine-control and client behavior first,
- search/connector trust next,
- core runtime and FTUI after shared state surfaces are real,
- rendering/backend parity after higher-risk control paths,
- mux/SSH operational gaps in parallel where isolated.

Future-self rule: if a new suspect appears while working these beads, either attach it to the appropriate child bead or create a new child under this epic immediately. Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":3545,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure ledger: All 8 children closed. ft-akx00.1 (closure ledger), ft-akx00.2 (machine-control/SDK), ft-akx00.3 (operator-support/persistence), ft-akx00.4 (connector/search), ft-akx00.5 (runtime/scrollback/recording/FTUI), ft-akx00.6 (rendering/window backends), ft-akx00.7 (tmux/mux-domain/SSH), ft-akx00.8 (shared verification contract). Full placeholder-remediation program complete.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:55:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.1","title":"Maintain closure ledger for placeholder-remediation program","description":"# Purpose
Maintain the closure ledger for this remediation program so the audit remains complete and falsifiable.

This bead owns the suspect matrix, scope decisions, and final proof that every audited placeholder ended in a deliberate outcome. It exists to prevent the classic failure mode where implementation starts but the inventory stops being tracked.

# Deliverables
- A suspect-by-suspect ledger mapping each audited item to a child bead and final disposition.
- Explicit documentation for any item that gets reclassified as intentional or out-of-scope.
- Final cross-check before the parent epic closes.","design":"Treat the original audit as an immutable input set. For each suspect, record:
- exact file/function/branch,
- why it was considered placeholder behavior,
- which remediation bead owns it,
- what proof is required to declare it closed.

If a task discovers that the optimal fix is deletion or API collapse, record that explicitly here so future readers know it was a principled simplification rather than an omission.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ledger names every verified suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit.
- Each suspect points to exactly one owning child bead or an explicit documented reclassification.
- The final comment on this bead reports any deltas between the initial inventory and the executed work.
- The ledger records not only ownership but also the verification surface for each audited placeholder: unit tests, E2E/integration coverage, and artifact expectations.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageHorse","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.899175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.898914Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2741,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Initial ledger categories:
- Control plane/client: NTM stub response, client workspace/unilateral handling, client spawn/alt-screen, robot SDK transport.
- Operator support: chrono stub, env-bootstrap/ringlog stubs, scheduled backup compression.
- Search/connectors: transparency verification, certification probe, reranker, model2vec, fastembed sentinel behavior.
- Core runtime/UX: NullPaneScrollbackAccess, recording encodings, FTUI placeholder views, stale PaneCapabilities stub comment.
- Rendering/backends: Wayland seat+keyboard, WGL/macOS/EGL resize, bitmap/WebGPU readback, terminfo raw/crop images, background sizing, custom glyph placeholders.
- Mux/SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle, detach semantics, SFTP metadata, SSH Match Exec.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Inventory-to-bead ledger for the 2026-04-02 placeholder audit:

Control plane / client / SDK
- `build_ntm_stub_response` fake `"status": "stub"` payload -> `ft-akx00.2.1`
- `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO + unilateral-message FIXME -> `ft-akx00.2.2`
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` hardcoded false -> `ft-akx00.2.3`
- generated Rust SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` -> `ft-akx00.2.4`

Operator support / persistence utilities
- `chrono_stub_now()` -> `ft-akx00.3.1`
- `env-bootstrap` / `ringlog` stub + no-op logger -> `ft-akx00.3.2`
- scheduled backup compression placeholder -> `ft-akx00.3.3`

Connector / search
- connector transparency verification stub -> `ft-akx00.4.1`
- certification integration probe stub -> `ft-akx00.4.2`
- `CrossEncoderReranker` identity/no-op stub -> `ft-akx00.4.3`
- `Model2VecEmbedder` stub + FastEmbed sentinel constructor -> `ft-akx00.4.4`

Core runtime / FTUI / policy
- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` placeholder -> `ft-akx00.5.1`
- `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `Repeat` placeholders -> `ft-akx00.5.2`
- FTUI placeholder view bodies -> `ft-akx00.5.3`
- stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation in `policy.rs` -> `ft-akx00.5.4`

Rendering / windowing
- Wayland seat + keyboard TODO/unimplemented paths -> `ft-akx00.6.1`
- WGL/macOS/EGL resize + framebuffer gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.2`
- bitmap/WebGPU texture readback gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.3`
- terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.4`
- GUI background sizing + custom glyph placeholder rendering -> `ft-akx00.6.5`

Mux / SSH
- tmux PTY `try_wait()` + kill placeholders -> `ft-akx00.7.1`
- detach unimplemented across domains -> `ft-akx00.7.2`
- SFTP metadata mutation placeholder -> `ft-akx00.7.3`
- SSH `Match Exec` omission -> `ft-akx00.7.4`

Deliberate exclusions from this epic unless later reclassified by evidence:
- test-only fake panes and other `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`
- unsupported-platform no-op shims used only to represent platform absence
- vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:17Z"},{"id":2798,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification-matrix reminder:
- Shared contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`
- Every concrete child under `ft-akx00` now requires both comprehensive unit tests and a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario with structured logs and artifacts.
- Completion evidence for future implementation beads should cite the exact proposed script name (or renamed final equivalent), exact commands, artifact root, and whether heavy validation ran via `rch`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"},{"id":2813,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"SageHorse","text":"Ledger update for `ft-akx00.5.2` (closed 2026-04-03):

Suspect:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`: dormant `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `DeltaEncoding::Repeat` placeholder branches plus `DiffOp` placeholder type.

Disposition:
- Closed by API collapse, not by implementing fake delta machinery. Recording WAR v1 now exposes only `DeltaEncoding::Full(Vec<u8>)`, and the spec was updated to say so explicitly. This is a principled narrowing of scope, not an omission.

Proof captured:
- Code/docs updated: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs`, `docs/recording-format-spec.md`.
- Regression: serde test now rejects the removed legacy placeholder variants.
- Targeted verification passed: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_recording` (47 tests).
- Local formatting proof: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_reranker.rs`.

Verification notes / non-bead blockers:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` remains blocked by remote worker env drift (`x11-xcb.pc` missing on worker `vmi1149989`).
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` remains blocked by existing failures in `frankenterm/config/derive` and `frankenterm/dynamic/derive`.
- Fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets` got past earlier test exhaustiveness drift and now fails only on unrelated bench debt in `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/input_latency_bench.rs` and `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/spike_comparison.rs`.

Cross-reference:
- Owning bead: `ft-akx00.5.2`
- Verification contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`

Follow-up for this ledger bead: accumulate the same disposition/proof block for each remaining child bead so the parent epic can be closed against a falsifiable matrix rather than scattered thread history.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:25:13Z"},{"id":3541,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"# ft-akx00 Closure Ledger — Final Cross-Check (FoggyRobin, 2026-04-19)

## Deliberate Exclusions (unchanged from initial audit)

## Gap Analysis
**Only remaining gap**: ft-akx00.6.3 (texture readback across bitmap/WebGPU) — currently in_progress, assigned to jemanuel. Once this closes, ft-akx00.6 epic and then the parent ft-akx00 epic can close.

**No orphaned items**: Every suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit maps to exactly one child bead. No suspects were lost or reclassified without documentation.

**Infrastructure blockers noted but not code-level**: Several rch workers lack platform toolchains (wayland-client.pc, x11.pc, Windows/macOS cross-CC). These limit CI verification breadth but do not indicate missing code.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
